titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Nanking , January 15, 1949—2 p. m.
[Received January 15—1:49 p. m.]

div.p:
120. On evening 13 January, following delivery our aide-mémoire,See telegram No. 113, January 14, 6 p. m., p. 47. Generalissimo met with Premier,Sun Fo, president of the Executive Yuan and son of the late Sun Yat-sen. Vice-Premier,Wu Te-chen, concurrently Minister for Foreign Affairs. Shao Li-tze,Kuomintang member and Chinese Ambassador in the Soviet Union, 1939–42.
Chang Chun,Military and Political Affairs Director for Southwest China. Chang Chih-chungMilitary and Political Affairs Director for Northwest China and Government representative during the 1946 negotiations with the Chinese Communist Party. and Chen Li-fu.Chinese Minister without Portfolio and coleader of the so-called C-C clique in the Kuomintang. In view nature our communication, last three named were directed seek direct approach to Communists. Generalissimo’s departure was also discussed. Indications suggest he may intend leave soon, going Foochow and Amoy and then Taiwan.
